title: cytisus subspinescens var. candidus
intro: Cytisus subspinescens var. candidus, also known as the White Broom or White Scotch Broom, is a flowering plant belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Cytisus subspinescens var. candidus are attributed to its flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been shown to offer health benefits such as reducing anxiety, improving sleep, and alleviating symptoms of menopause. In herbal preparations, the plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and support overall well-being.
